The classification of Geraniospermum elongatum is based on its morphological characteristics and molecular phylogenetic analysis.

intro:
There is no information available on "Geraniospermum elongatum". It seems that this plant does not exist.

However, if you are referring to "Geranium" or "Geraniospermum", it's possible you're thinking of Geranium elatum or a similar species. 

Geranium elatum is a plant species native to Europe. It is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the geranium family.

This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can aid in wound healing and reduce pain.

The bioactive constituents of Geranium elatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.

The most common herbal preparations made with Geranium elatum include tinctures, infusions, and topical creams, which are used to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations and wounds.
